samples.xml_description:
<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for edge detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="state0">Initial state.</parameter>
        <parameter name="state1">State when rising edge detected.</parameter>
        <parameter name="state2">State when falling edge detected.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="current_state">Current state.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="next_state">Next state.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="rise_reg">Rising edge output register.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="down_reg">Falling edge output register.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The edge_detect module detects rising and falling edges in the input signal a and generates corresponding output signals rise and down. 
        <sequential>
            On the positive edge of the clock signal clk or the negative edge of the reset signal rst_n, the current state is updated. 
            If the reset signal is active (low), the current state is set to state0 (initial state). 
            Otherwise, the current state is updated to the next state.
        </sequential>
        <combinational>
            The next state is determined based on the current state and the input signal a. 
            If the current state is state0 and the input signal a is 0, the next state remains state0. 
            If the input signal a is 1, the next state is state1 (rising edge detected). 
            If the current state is state1 and the input signal a is 1, the next state is state2 (falling edge detected). 
            If the input signal a is 0, the next state remains state2. 
            If the current state is state2 and the input signal a is 0, the next state is state0. 
            If the input signal a is 1, the next state is state1 (rising edge detected).
        </combinational>
        <combinational>
            The rise_reg and down_reg outputs are updated based on the current state. 
            If the current state is state1, the rise_reg is set to 1, indicating a rising edge. 
            If the current state is state2, the down_reg is set to 1, indicating a falling edge. 
            In all other cases, both registers are set to 0.
        </combinational>
        <sequential>
            The rise and down output signals are updated based on the rise_reg and down_reg registers. 
            If the rise_reg is 1, the rise output signal is set to 1, indicating a rising edge. 
            If the down_reg is 1, the down output signal is set to 1, indicating a falling edge. 
            In all other cases, both output signals are set to 0.
        </sequential>
        <sequential>
            The rise and down output signals are updated based on the rise_reg and down_reg registers. 
            If the rise_reg is 1, the rise output signal is set to 1, indicating a rising edge. 
            If the down_reg is 1, the down output signal is set to 1, indicating a falling edge. 
            In all other cases, both output signals are set to 0.
        </sequential>
    </implementation>
</module>
